findings.title: Competitive landscape has EXPLODED — 12+ competitors now, including 3 new threats since last research
findings.description:
The estate settlement space is far more crowded than originally assessed. Key updates as of March 2026:

**Tier 1 — Direct Threats (free or cheap, similar model):**
1. **Sunset** (hellosunset.com) — FREE. iPhone + Android apps. Nationwide coverage: all 50 states, 3000+ counties. Finds most assets in 1 business day. FDIC-insured estate account up to $3M. SOC 2 Type II certified. Revenue from bank partner interest. MOST DANGEROUS competitor since also free and has mobile apps.
2. **SwiftProbate** (swiftprobate.com) — $39 one-time. 3,200+ county-specific probate guides + 160+ institution guides. Already doing programmatic SEO for probate content at county level. Strong competitor on SEO front.
3. **Atticus** (weareatticus.com) — iOS-only (no Android, no web app). Step-by-step guide, estate inventory, shareable reports. Won Fast Company World Changing Idea. Already ranks for 'what to do when loved one dies 2026 checklist'.

**Tier 2 — Premium competitors:**
4. **Empathy** (empathy.com) — $162M raised. 45M+ lives covered via B2B insurance partnerships. BMO Insurance partnership Jan 2026. LifeVault reached 2M users in 2 months. Massive but B2B-locked.
5. **Elayne** (elayne.com) — Flat fee starting ~$250. AI + human support. Searches 50+ financial categories. Good content/SEO play.
6. **EverSettled** (eversettled.com) — $199 per estate. State-level guidance, document organization, optional estate specialists.
7. **EstateExec** — $199. Focus on executor accounting and tracking. Won 2026 ACQ5 Global Awards.
8. **Alix** — 1% of estate, $9K minimum. High-end full-service.
9. **ClearEstate** — 1.75%, $20K min. Primarily Canadian.

**Tier 3 — Niche/adjacent:**
10. **Cadence** — Software + live Certified Executor Advisors.
11. **Caily** (caily.com) — Post-death account closure and subscription cancellation guidance. Content-focused.
12. **Legacy Logix** — AI document sorting, task identification, deadline alerts.
13. **SubsVault** (subsvault.com) — Digital legacy planning, subscription management after death.

**NEW since last research:** SwiftProbate (county-level SEO threat), EverSettled ($199 competitor), Caily (subscription cancellation content), SubsVault (digital legacy), SwiftProbate's 3,200+ county guides.
findings.suggested_action: Update CLAUDE.md competitive landscape section with all new competitors. AfterLoss differentiation must be razor-sharp: (1) 100% free forever (beats Atticus $15/mo, EverSettled $199, SwiftProbate $39, Elayne $250), (2) Web-first with no app download required (beats Atticus iOS-only, Sunset app-only), (3) AI-generated personalized documents via Claude API (no competitor has this), (4) Programmatic SEO at state level with interactive tool (beats SwiftProbate's static guides). Sunset is #1 threat since it's also free — but AfterLoss differentiates on AI doc gen, broader checklist scope, and empathy-first UX vs Sunset's focus on asset discovery/probate docs.

findings.title: SwiftProbate is already doing county-level programmatic SEO — AfterLoss must go state-level fast
findings.description:
SwiftProbate (swiftprobate.com) has 3,200+ county-specific probate guides with URLs like /probate/{state}/{county}. They have comparison pages (/compare/elayne, /compare/estateexec, etc.) and blog content. Atticus already ranks for 'what to do when loved one dies 2026 checklist' at weareatticus.com/articles/what-to-do-when-loved-one-dies.

Current SEO landscape for target keywords:
- 'what to do when someone dies checklist' — AARP, Fidelity, Atticus, Trust & Will, Ever Loved all competing. All are static articles. No interactive tool ranks.
- 'probate process [state]' — SwiftProbate ranking for county-level pages. Elayne has state resources pages.
- 'how to close bank account after death' — Elayne, Everplans, generic articles.
- 'cancel subscriptions after death' — Elayne, Caily, Everplans competing.

AfterLoss strategy: Go state-level (not county) for MVP — 51 pages × 3 topics = 153 pages. Beat static articles with interactive tool. Capture 'what to do when someone dies' with comprehensive interactive checklist that outperforms static PDFs.
findings.suggested_action: Priority: Build 153 programmatic SEO pages (51 states × probate + small-estate + death-certificate). Add JSON-LD structured data per Next.js 15 best practices. Use generateStaticParams() for SSG. Add sitemap.ts in app directory. Each state page should have unique data: probate threshold, small estate limit, filing deadline, court website, downloadable forms URL, fees. This is the highest-leverage SEO work.

findings.title: Wave is CONFIRMED and STRENGTHENING — CBO projects deaths > births by 2030
findings.description:
Key demographic updates as of March 2026:
- CBO projects deaths will exceed births by 2030 (Fortune, Jan 2026)
- US life expectancy hit record 79 years in 2024 (US News, Jan 2026)
- Age-adjusted death rate decreased 3.8% (2023→2024) per CDC DB548
- But TOTAL deaths increasing due to population aging — baby boomers turning 80 in 2026
- Deaths projected at 3.6M/yr now, rising to peak in 2055
- Senior share: 18.7% (2025) → 23% (2050)
- Digital estate complexity growing: subscriptions, crypto, social media, digital purchases

The market is validated and growing. The $162M raised by Empathy alone proves venture interest. AfterLoss's free model is the right play for direct-to-consumer SEO acquisition.
findings.suggested_action: Use demographic data in SEO content strategy. Target adult children of aging boomers (primary user persona: 45-65 years old, managing parents' affairs). Mention growing digital estate complexity as content angle.

findings.title: State probate thresholds have changed recently — data curation must track changes
findings.description:
Recent changes discovered:
- California: Small estate affidavit threshold increased to $208,850 (April 1, 2025 via AB 2016). Also new: primary residence transfer without full probate up to $750K gross value. Small Estate Set-Asides increased to $107,900.
- Illinois: Probate Act amended August 15, 2025 — small estate limit raised from $100K to $150K. Vehicles can now be transferred regardless of other asset values.

These changes confirm that state probate rules are a LIVING dataset that must be kept current. The data must be versioned and include 'last_verified' dates.

Data sources for curation:
- Justia 50-state survey: https://www.justia.com/probate/probate-administration/small-estates/small-estates-laws-and-procedures-50-state-survey/
- Nolo state guides: https://www.nolo.com/legal-encyclopedia/estate-planning-your-state
- SmallEstateAffidavit.com: https://smallestateaffidavit.com/small-estate-affidavit-limits/
- SwiftProbate state pages (competitor reference): https://www.swiftprobate.com/probate/{state}
- Elayne resources: https://www.elayne.com/resources/
findings.suggested_action: When building state probate rules database (issue #253), include a 'last_verified_date' column. Create a quarterly data refresh workflow. Start with the 10 most populous states: CA, TX, FL, NY, PA, IL, OH, GA, NC, MI. Include the 2025 changes for CA and IL.

findings.title: @react-pdf/renderer has server-side limitations in Next.js App Router — need workaround
findings.description:
Research confirms @react-pdf/renderer cannot easily render PDFs server-side in Next.js App Router route handlers due to missing browser APIs (DOMMatrix not defined). Options:

1. **Client-side generation with dynamic imports** (recommended for MVP): Use dynamic(() => import('@react-pdf/renderer'), { ssr: false }). Generate PDF in browser, return as download. Simpler, works immediately.
2. **Server-side via API route with polyfills**: Requires canvas and other Node.js polyfills. More complex but keeps PDF generation server-side.
3. **Alternative: jsPDF** for server-side: Simpler API, works in Node.js, but less React-native.

Recommendation: Use client-side generation for MVP. The architecture doc says serverExternalPackages config is needed, but the reality is client-side dynamic import is more reliable. The letter text comes from Claude API (server-side), but PDF rendering happens client-side.

Next.js 15 SSG/ISR best practices for programmatic SEO:
- Use generateStaticParams() for state pages
- Add sitemap.ts in app directory using MetadataRoute.Sitemap
- Add JSON-LD structured data for rich snippets
- Use Metadata API for dynamic title/description per page
- ISR revalidation: 7 days for state data, 30 days for templates
findings.suggested_action: Update architecture doc to reflect client-side PDF generation as MVP approach. Configure next.config.ts with serverExternalPackages as a fallback. When implementing doc generation (issue #115), use dynamic import pattern for @react-pdf/renderer.

findings.title: Scaffold has 9+ known learnings violations (issue #252 exists but not yet fixed)
findings.description:
The afterloss scaffold (PR #213) was merged with violations already tracked in issue #252:
1. ESLint config extends 'next/typescript' (deprecated) — should be 'next/core-web-vitals' only
2. .env.example has TEST_MODE=true (should be false)
3. E2E tests repeat page.goto('/') instead of using test.beforeEach
4. tsconfig.json excludes e2e from type checking (should include)
5. Playwright config hardcodes baseURL (should use env var fallback)
6. globals.css uses corporate blue (#2563eb) instead of empathetic warm colors per architecture docs
7. font-family override in globals.css (should let Tailwind handle)
8. Missing /api/health route
9. Missing Prettier configuration
10. Missing ESLint data-testid enforcement rule
11. env.ts getter error-throwing behavior untested
findings.suggested_action: Issue #252 must be the FIRST implementation task. Fix all 11 violations in a single PR. This establishes a clean foundation before building features.

findings.title: AfterLoss's #1 differentiator is being web-first AND free — Atticus is iOS-only, Sunset is app-only
findings.description:
Critical competitive insight: Atticus is currently iOS-only (working on Android). Sunset requires downloading an app (iPhone + Android). No competitor offers a full-featured web-first experience that works immediately in a browser without download or account creation.

AfterLoss's web-first approach means:
- Instant access from Google search (no app download friction)
- Works on any device (desktop, tablet, phone)
- No account required (localStorage-first)
- Indexable by search engines (programmatic SEO)
- Progressive Web App (PWA) potential for future offline access

This is the strongest positioning: 'Free, instant, works right now in your browser.' No download. No account. No subscription. Just help when you need it most.
findings.suggested_action: Emphasize web-first positioning in landing page copy and SEO content. Add PWA manifest for future offline support. The no-download, no-account, no-subscription message should be front and center.

findings.title: Subscription cancellation templates are validated as major pain point — Elayne and Caily already have content
findings.description:
Multiple competitors are addressing post-death subscription cancellation:
- Elayne has a detailed guide: 'How to Cancel Digital Subscriptions After Someone Dies'
- Caily has comprehensive blog: 'Accounts and Subscriptions to Cancel After a Death'
- Everplans has guides on closing online accounts
- SubsVault offers digital legacy planning for subscription management

Common documentation required: death certificate, proof of executor/administrator status, photo ID, account email/phone.

Key categories to cover (100+ services):
- Streaming: Netflix, Hulu, Disney+, YouTube TV, Apple TV+, Spotify, Amazon Prime, HBO Max, Peacock, Paramount+
- Social: Facebook (memorialization vs deletion), Instagram, X/Twitter, LinkedIn, TikTok, Reddit, Pinterest
- Financial: Banks (varies by institution), credit cards, investment accounts, crypto exchanges, PayPal, Venmo
- Insurance: Auto, home, health, life (claim vs cancel), dental, vision
- Utilities: Electric, gas, water, internet, phone, cable
- Subscriptions: Gym, magazines, meal kits, Adobe, Microsoft 365, iCloud, Google One, Amazon Prime, Costco
- Government: SSA, DMV, voter registration, Medicare/Medicaid, VA benefits
- Memberships: AAA, warehouse clubs, professional associations, unions

AfterLoss differentiator: Generate actual cancellation LETTERS via Claude API, not just guides. Each letter personalized with deceased name, account info, executor details.
findings.suggested_action: Build subscription cancellation database as Supabase table (issue #256). Seed with 100+ services. Each entry: service_name, category, cancellation_method, contact_info, required_docs, processing_time, letter_template_prompt. This data powers both the checklist feature and SEO pages (/templates/cancel-{service}-after-death).

findings.title: Affiliate revenue model validated — LegalZoom 15%, Trust & Will 20%, BetterHelp $10-40/referral
findings.description:
Validated affiliate programs for AfterLoss:
- LegalZoom: 15% commission per sale ($125+). Estate planning bundles. Via CJ Affiliate.
- Trust & Will: 20% commission, avg $80/sale, up to $180. Via Impact.com. Strong fit — users experiencing the process often want to create their OWN estate plan.
- BetterHelp: $10-$40 per referral (CPA model). Grief counseling. Contextual at grief support steps.
- VitalChek: Exclusive online partner for 450+ vital records offices. No formal affiliate program found — may need direct partnership outreach. Potential referral for death certificate ordering.

NOTE: Affiliate placement must be tasteful and contextual per CLAUDE.md UX principles. Show at relevant checklist steps only. Clearly labeled. Never intrusive.
findings.suggested_action: Apply to LegalZoom (CJ Affiliate) and Trust & Will (Impact.com) affiliate programs early — approval can take 2-4 weeks. Implement affiliate integration (issue #124) after core checklist is built. Contact VitalChek about potential referral partnership for death certificate ordering.

findings.title: Anonymous-first RLS pattern validated by ScoreRebound PR #257 — apply to AfterLoss Supabase schema
findings.description:
PR #257 (ScoreRebound database schema) was the first product to implement anonymous-first UX with Supabase RLS. All 3 code reviewers found critical bugs:
1. Anonymous INSERT without matching SELECT → users can create but not read data
2. UPDATE policies blocking row claiming after signup (NULL = anything is never true in SQL)

These exact bugs will apply to AfterLoss's database schema since it also uses anonymous-first with optional Supabase Auth. The learnings from PR #257 are already in .ralph/learnings.md.

When building AfterLoss's Supabase schema (issue #111), the implementer MUST:
- Add SELECT policies for anon role matching every INSERT policy
- Use 'auth.uid() = user_id OR user_id IS NULL' in UPDATE policies for claimable rows
- Include session_id column for anonymous session tracking
- Test RLS policies explicitly in the migration or seed file
findings.suggested_action: When issue #111 (Supabase schema) is assigned, the worker MUST read .ralph/learnings.md entries about anonymous RLS policies. Include RLS test assertions in the migration. This learning is already documented — just ensure it's applied.

findings.title: No CI/CD pipeline exists — issue #127 open but low priority, should be elevated
findings.description:
AfterLoss has no CI/CD workflow (.github/workflows/ci-afterloss.yml does not exist). This means:
- No automated build/test/lint on PRs
- No automated deployment to Vercel preview
- Code review bots may not trigger correctly

Other products have CI workflows. AfterLoss should get one ASAP to catch regressions as features are built.
findings.suggested_action: Elevate issue #127 from P3 to P2. Create CI workflow that runs: bun install → bun run lint → bun run build → bun run test. Trigger on changes to afterloss/** on PRs targeting main.

findings.title: Color palette mismatch — scaffold uses corporate blue, architecture doc specifies sage green/warm tones
findings.description:
globals.css currently uses:
- --primary: #2563eb (corporate blue)
- --primary-hover: #1d4ed8
- --background: #ffffff (stark white)
- --foreground: #171717 (near-black)

Architecture doc (docs/architecture.md lines 188-197) specifies:
- Primary: Sage green or warm gray (calming, not clinical)
- Accent: Soft gold (gentle warmth)
- Background: Off-white / warm cream (not stark white)
- Text: Dark warm gray (not pure black)
- WCAG 2.1 AA compliant (many users are elderly 70+)

Competitor color analysis:
- Empathy: warm tones, soft gradients
- Atticus: blue-green palette
- Sunset: warm amber/orange accents
- Elayne: clean white with green accents

The landing page redesign (issue #255) should fix this.
findings.suggested_action: Issue #255 (landing page redesign) must include color palette update. Suggested palette: sage green primary (#6B8F71), soft gold accent (#C9A961), warm cream background (#FAF8F5), dark warm gray text (#3D3D3D). All must pass WCAG 2.1 AA contrast ratio checks.

findings.title: JSON-LD structured data and sitemap.ts are required for Next.js 15 SEO — not yet implemented
findings.description:
Next.js 15 best practices for programmatic SEO (per 2026 guides):
1. Metadata API: dynamic title/description per page using generateMetadata()
2. sitemap.ts in /app directory using MetadataRoute.Sitemap API
3. JSON-LD structured data for FAQPage, HowTo, Article schema types
4. Open Graph tags for social sharing
5. Canonical URLs to prevent duplicate content
6. robots.ts for crawl directives

The landing page has basic metadata but lacks structured data, sitemap, and programmatic page metadata.

For estate/probate content, relevant schema types:
- FAQPage: state probate FAQ pages
- HowTo: step-by-step checklist guides
- Article: informational guides
- BreadcrumbList: navigation hierarchy
findings.suggested_action: Add sitemap.ts and robots.ts to the app directory. When building programmatic state pages, include generateMetadata() with state-specific title/description and JSON-LD structured data. This should be part of the programmatic SEO pages implementation.

findings.title: Consider PWA support for offline access to checklists and saved documents
findings.description:
Since AfterLoss is web-first and uses localStorage, adding Progressive Web App (PWA) support would enable:
- Offline access to the checklist and saved progress
- Install to home screen (compete with Atticus and Sunset's native apps)
- Push notifications for deadline reminders (with account)
- Works on all platforms without app store submission

This is a future enhancement, not MVP-blocking.
findings.suggested_action: After core features are built, add next-pwa or @ducanh2912/next-pwa for PWA support. Add manifest.json with app icons. This makes AfterLoss installable without app store friction.

findings.title: E2E tests only cover landing page — need coverage for onboarding, checklist, document generation flows
findings.description:
Current Playwright smoke tests (e2e/smoke.spec.ts) only verify landing page elements. As features are built, E2E tests must cover:
1. Onboarding flow: state selection → relationship → estate complexity
2. Checklist interaction: expand items, check off tasks, see progress update
3. localStorage persistence: complete tasks → refresh → verify persistence
4. Document generation: fill form → generate → verify PDF download
5. State guide pages: navigate to state, verify correct probate data
6. Affiliate links: visible at correct checklist steps, clearly labeled

Every new feature PR should include corresponding Playwright tests.
findings.suggested_action: Include Playwright test requirements in every new feature issue's acceptance criteria. Use data-testid consistently. Create test user scenarios in TEST_MODE.

stack_recommendation.rationale: Stack is CONFIRMED correct. Next.js 15 App Router with SSG/ISR is ideal for 153+ programmatic SEO pages. Supabase for optional user progress sync, state probate rules database, and generated document storage. Supabase Auth for optional accounts (localStorage-first by default). Vercel for fast SSG builds with ISR revalidation. Add: zustand (state management with persist middleware), @react-pdf/renderer (client-side PDF generation via dynamic import), @anthropic-ai/sdk (Claude API for document generation). NOTE: @react-pdf/renderer should use client-side rendering via dynamic import, not server-side in route handlers.

apis_discovered.name: Justia 50-State Probate Survey
apis_discovered.notes: Comprehensive state-by-state small estate laws. Web scraping or manual data entry needed. Primary reference for seeding state_probate_rules table.

apis_discovered.notes: State-specific estate planning law summaries. Reference for state guide content.

apis_discovered.notes: Quick reference for small estate affidavit thresholds by state. Cross-reference with state court websites.

apis_discovered.notes: 3,200+ county-specific probate guides. Useful as reference for data accuracy but do NOT copy content. Use as a cross-reference for state probate data.

apis_discovered.name: SSA Form SSA-721
apis_discovered.notes: Statement of Death by Funeral Director form. Reference for government forms guidance.

apis_discovered.notes: 20% commission, avg $80/sale, up to $180. Via Impact.com. Strong fit for users who want to create their own estate plan after experiencing the process.

apis_discovered.notes: $10-$40 per qualified referral (CPA). Grief counseling specialization. Contextual at grief support checklist steps.
